_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass (SWORD VARIANT) — take the L5 'Sergeant' composited keeper and produce the L6 'Veteran Sergeant' variant (Tier 2 top).
history: v1 (2026-05-07): generated by build_sword_prompts.py from `infantry_edit_L5_to_L6.json` (mace variant). Bulk language substitutions: mace→sword, wooden haft→leather grip, iron spikes→cross-guard, mace head→sword blade, etc. Sword-specific weapon beats applied at L3, L4, L5, L6, L7, L10 (see WEAPON_BEAT_OVERRIDES in build_sword_prompts.py for the per-level deltas).
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chained (Rule 2): two inputs = L5 composite + L1_Sword_Base scale anchor.

prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The first input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the L5 'Sergeant' kit accumulated on top of the L1 base. You MUST preserve every L2 + L3 + L4 + L5 feature shown in the first input image: (a) the existing PARTIAL STEEL BREASTPLATE on the upper chest (the new L6 step EXTENDS this breastplate downward — it does not replace it; the existing upper-chest steel reads as a continuous piece with the bevor at L5); (b) the WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle; (c) the BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH with flap and brass stud on the anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT) — the new L6 tassets cover the upper thigh BELOW the belt; the hip pouch hangs from the belt at the waist and remains visible at the upper edge of the tasset on that side; (d) the BROWN LEATHER KNIFE SHEATH with cream bone-handle knife on the anatomical-LEFT hip (viewer-RIGHT) — same arrangement: sheath hangs from the belt and remains visible at the upper edge of the tasset on that side; (e) TWO STEEL PAULDRON CAPS (the new L6 step ADDS a smaller second disc on top of each existing pauldron); (f) STEEL VAMBRACES on both forearms wrapping wrist to mid-forearm; (g) BRASS QUILLON CAPS at the cross-guard tips (small warm-brass bulb caps at each end of the cross-guard, distinctly warm brass tone against the cool steel cross-guard) — these MUST REMAIN at this level; (h) FOUR IRON STRAP-BANDS radiating from the central steel boss in a cross pattern on the steel-faced shield; (i) STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD with continuous dark-iron rim band, eight L2 rim rivets, and the central cool-gray steel boss; (j) the L4 LONGER CROSS-GUARD (~5" wide) WITH DOWNWARD-CURLED QUILLON TIPS plus the DEEPENED CENTRAL FULLER on the sword blade — these MUST REMAIN at this level plus the L5 heavier wheel pommel (replacing the L1 plain disc pommel); (k) the SCALE-MAIL SHORT SLEEVES on both upper arms (chunky overlapping rounded scales) — these stay where they are at the bicep level. DO NOT revert any of these features. The second input image is provided ONLY as a scale and framing reference — match its character-to-canvas proportions exactly; do NOT apply any of its content. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the first input image, marking the top of Tier 2 (Veteran Sergeant). The unit's silhouette becomes visibly bulkier — the breastplate fills the abdomen, layered pauldrons broaden the shoulders, tassets flare at the hips, and steel sabatons articulate the foot. The livery is now reduced to a narrow skirt band at the waist. (1) FULL BREASTPLATE EXTENSION DOWNWARD TO THE WAIST BELT — extend the existing L5 partial steel breastplate downward to cover the entire abdomen all the way to the wide leather belt. The result is a single continuous cool silver-gray polished steel breastplate covering the front of the torso from collarbone (where it joins the bevor at the throat) down to the belt line at the waist. The breastplate has a slight medial ridge running vertically down the center from collar to belt. The lower edge of the breastplate is a clean horizontal line that sits flush with the top edge of the wide leather belt. The livery tunic chest is now FULLY COVERED by the extended breastplate — the red+mustard split tunic is no longer visible above the belt at the chest. The livery only remains visible as a narrow SKIRT BAND BELOW the belt (per Edit Goal item 2 below). The breastplate is one continuous piece — no horizontal seam between upper and lower halves. (2) STEEL TASSETS HANGING FROM THE BELT AT BOTH HIPS — add two short rectangular steel plates hanging from the wide leather belt at each hip, one on each side. Each tasset is approximately 6 inches tall and 5 inches wide, cool silver-gray polished steel matching the breastplate, with a slight outward flare at the lower edge so the bottom corners are wider than the top corners. The tassets cover the upper thigh below the belt. The two tassets are separated at the front by a vertical gap of approximately 3 inches — through this gap the LIVERY TUNIC SKIRT remains visible as a narrow band of red+mustard split cloth between the belt (top edge) and the lower edge of the tassets (the front gap shows the red+mustard skirt extending down to mid-thigh below the tassets). Each tasset has two visible dark-iron rivets at its top edge where it riveted to a hidden leather strap that loops over the belt. The existing brown leather hip pouch (anatomical-RIGHT) and brown leather knife sheath (anatomical-LEFT) remain hanging from the belt and are visible at the upper edge of each tasset on its side, sitting in front of the tasset (the pouch and sheath are not hidden by the tassets). (3) LAYERED 2-DISC PAULDRONS (silhouette mightiness rule — visibly broader shoulder line than at L5) — add a smaller second disc layered ON TOP of each existing steel pauldron cap, riveted to the original. Each pauldron now reads as two stacked discs: the original L4/L5 base disc (~5 inches diameter) and a smaller upper disc (~3.5 inches diameter) sitting on top of it like a stacked pauldron. Both discs are cool silver-gray polished steel; the upper disc has two visible dark-iron rivets at its lower seam where it joins the base disc. The shoulder envelope reads visibly broader than at L5 — the silhouette must clearly increase in mass at the shoulder line. (4) STEEL SABATONS OVER THE LEATHER BOOTS — add cool silver-gray steel toe caps and steel ankle straps over the existing warm-brown leather boots. Each sabaton consists of: (i) a steel toe cap covering the front third of the foot from toe to instep, smooth domed steel; (ii) a steel ankle strap wrapping horizontally around the ankle just above the boot's folded cuff, approximately 1 inch tall, with a small dark-iron buckle on the outer side. The boots themselves remain warm-brown leather visible at the calf, heel, and the central foot area between the toe cap and the ankle strap. Two visible dark-iron rivets at the seam between the toe cap and the boot leather. (5) TWO STEEL GRIP-RINGS AT THE TOP AND BOTTOM OF THE LEATHER GRIP — add two thin steel collar rings at the leather grip: one at the seam where the grip meets the cross-guard (top), one at the seam where the grip meets the wheel pommel (bottom). Each ring is approximately ½ inch tall, dark cool-gray steel, riveted with two tiny dark-iron rivets each. The leather wrap of the grip remains warm-brown leather visible BETWEEN the two rings; the rings are small steel collars, NOT a full sleeve.  ===== PRESERVE FROM THE FIRST INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the first input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline; his stern flat closed-mouth expression; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with single bright catchlight; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin with rosy blush; his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering chin and lower cheeks (still framed at the bottom by the L5 bevor / upper-chest steel; beard remains visible above the bevor's top edge) — trimmed short, NOT bushy, NOT wild; his dark mustache; his dark-brown short hair as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front of the mail coif; the mail coif covering scalp, ears, and back-of-neck drape (front-of-throat covered by the L5 bevor merged with the breastplate); the existing wide warm-brown leather belt with square dark-iron buckle (visible BETWEEN the new tassets at the front and through the gap); the existing brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip (visible at the upper edge of the right tasset); the existing brown leather knife sheath with bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip (visible at the upper edge of the left tasset); the existing leather grip wrapping the entire grip of the sword. ===== POSE (must match the first input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE SWORD ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, elbow bent ~120 degrees, hand grips the sword's leather grip, sword blade dangling at hip height angled down. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, forearm angled across the body, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height with face presenting toward viewer at slight three-quarter angle. Calm guard stance — both arms static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match the SECOND input image exactly — L1_Base scale anchor) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 66.8% of canvas vertical height (~684px tall), approximately 60.8% of canvas horizontal width (~623px wide). TOP of mail coif at ~16.6% from top of canvas. BOTTOM of boots at ~16.6% from bottom of canvas. Character vertically centered. ~18.3% empty margin viewer-LEFT, ~20.9% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT cover the LIVERY skirt band fully — the red+mustard split tunic skirt MUST remain visible as a narrow band between the belt (top) and the tasset bottom edge through the front gap between the two tassets; DO NOT shrink the livery skirt band to nothing or remove it entirely; DO NOT replace the livery tunic with a body coat, gambeson, surcoat, or any non-livery cloth; DO NOT extend the breastplate below the belt — the breastplate ends at the belt line at L6; DO NOT make the breastplate two pieces with a horizontal seam — single continuous piece from collar to belt; DO NOT make the tassets a single skirt-like fauld — they are TWO separate plates with a visible vertical gap at the front; DO NOT make the tassets longer than ~6 inches at L6 — extending tassets to mid-thigh arrives at L8; DO NOT add lames / vertical articulation lines to the tassets at L6 — they are single flat plates at L6 (3-lame articulated tassets arrive at L8); DO NOT make the layered pauldrons three or four discs — exactly TWO stacked discs at L6 (3-lame pauldrons arrive at L8, 4-lame at L10); DO NOT add gold rivets to the pauldrons — those arrive at L8; DO NOT add a third disc to the pauldrons; DO NOT make the sabatons cover the entire boot — the boots remain warm-brown leather visible at the calf and the heel and the central foot between toe cap and ankle strap; DO NOT extend sabatons up the calf to greaves — leg armor stays at the foot level at L6; DO NOT add greaves, knee plates, or any leg armor above the ankle strap; DO NOT replace the sword blade with a flanged steel head — that arrives at L7; DO NOT change the cross-guard shape on the sword blade; DO NOT remove the L4 deepened central blade fuller or the L5 wheel pommel; DO NOT make the sword grip reinforcement strip wrap the entire sword grip — it is a narrow vertical strip with wood visible on either side; DO NOT change the round shield to any non-round shape; DO NOT add embossed sun-burst boss or detailed boss pattern — those arrive at L7; DO NOT add a helm, sallet, kettle helm, or any structural addition to the head — head covering remains the existing mail coif (L7 introduces the open-face sallet); DO NOT add a closed visor, half-visor, or any face cover — face remains fully visible; DO NOT add gauntlets — gauntlets arrive at L7; DO NOT make the steel vambraces extend into gauntlets — vambraces stop at the wrist, hands remain bare; DO NOT add gold trim, gold rivets, gold band, gold buckle, gold accents, or any gold anywhere — first gold appears at L8; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT add horns, crests, plumes, or vertical helm extensions; DO NOT change any facial feature; DO NOT grow the beard bushy or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT remove the beard; DO NOT change the hair, the coif color, the trouser color, or the boot color (boots remain warm-brown leather where the sabatons do not cover); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the sword; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
